explanations.note: call meanings follow standard 2/1 Game Force

options.text: 4♥ — 4-25 HCP. Next call is usually Pass (66%); 4♠ (17%); Double (10%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (66%). A strong engine chooses this 22% of the time here. Where it leads: 4HN (66%), 4SW (11%), 5HXN (6%). The winner (EV gap >= 0.5 IMPs): +2.0 IMPs vs Pass (±0.7); wins on 50% of layouts against 37%.

Pass — limited — at most 14 HCP. Next call is usually 2♠ (30%); 3♣ (22%); Double (22%). A strong engine chooses this 45% of the time here. Where it leads: 3HN (22%), 2HN (16%), 4HN (11%). Scored -2.0 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.7); wins on 37% of layouts, pushes on 13%.

3♥ — preemptive — 4-9 HCP. Next call is usually Pass (37%); Double (24%); 3♠ (21%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (36%). A strong engine chooses this 52% of the time here. Where it leads: 3HN (36%), 3SW (13%), 5HXN (11%). Scored -3.8 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.7); wins on 32% of layouts, pushes on 5%.

classification.type_reason: With a fit and extra values opposite a weak two, the choice is between inviting (3H), signing off in game (4H), or passing—constructive valuation of how high to bid.
